page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-192-metal is a bare-metal server powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors using the x86_64 architecture. It features 192 dedicated vCPUs across 96 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running at a clock speed of 4.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 1.5 TB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple domains. The server achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, PassMark CPU scores, and PassMark database operations. Memory performance is highly competitive, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 13142.62 GB/sec and uncached large block read bandwidth at 464.93 GB/sec, both placing in the top 10%. Cryptographic and extended instruction tests also score in the top 10%, aided by hardware-accelerated instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX.

Given its bare-metal architecture and high memory-to-core ratio, this server is optimized for memory-intensive and compute-heavy enterprise workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale database operations, high-throughput Redis deployments, and software compilation. Additionally, the inclusion of Intel AMX tiles enables strong performance in CPU-based LLM inference, particularly for prompt processing and text generation on medium to large models. The bare-metal deployment model offers high resource density and eliminates hypervisor overhead, delivering strong qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring sustained, predictable hardware access.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-192-metal is a bare-metal server configured with 192 dedicated vCPUs using Intel Xeon 8481C processors running at 4.0 GHz. It features 1.5 TB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core, with no local storage or GPUs. The server delivers top-tier performance, placing in the top 10% for multi-core CPU benchmarks, database operations, and memory bandwidth. It supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which accelerate cryptographic tasks and CPU-based LLM inference. This bare-metal instance is highly suited for memory-intensive enterprise databases, high-throughput Redis deployments, software compilation, and large-scale data processing workloads that require maximum resource density without hypervisor overhead.

tagline: A high-density bare-metal server combining massive DDR5 memory capacity with top-tier multi-core processing for demanding enterprise database and compute workloads.

1.5 TB of high-speed DDR5 memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
